publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-01-07 00:13:23 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes correspondence, published material, and several manuscripts.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Jussawalla is an Indian poet, critic, and a man of letters. Together with Arvind Krishna Mehrotra he founded the Clearing House press that brought literary modernism to India.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, published material, and several manuscripts.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Audio visual media that has been digitized: TR-10929, TR-10930.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Adil Jussawalla Papers, #8519. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Related material located in collection #8511, Arvind Krishna Mehrotra papers.

list.head: Books - cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
list.item: Sarang, Vilas. Seven Critical Essays , Undated
Mahapatra, Jayanta. The False Start , 1980
Nazareth, H.O. Lobo , 1984
Menen, Rajendar. Nature Cure: Dr. J.M. Jussawalla's Drugless Healing , 2013

Ed. Bhalla, Alok; Verma, Nirmal; Ananthamurthy, U. R. Yatra 2 , 1994
Pritish Nandy, TR. The Poetry of Kaifi Azmi , 1975
Bandekar, Prakash. I am India' and a Few Other Poems , Undated
Chitre, Dilip; Jussawalla, Adil. The Reasoning Vision: Jehangir Sabavala's Painterly Universe , 1980
Nazareth, H.O. Lobo
Mehrotra, Arvind Krishna. Distance in Statute Miles
Lal, P. The Parrot's Death and Other Poems
de Souza, Eunice . Women in Dutch Painting

Patel, Gieve. Mister Behram
Mistry, Cyrus. Doongaji House
Shetty, Manohar. Borrowed Time
Shivdasani, Menka. Nirvana at Ten Rupees
Lal, P. Love's the First

Malgonkar, Manohar. Bombay Beware

Fernandes, Keith and Eunice de Souza (Editors) . Bequest
Opinion Literary Quarterly Winter Issue
Opinion Literary Quarterly Autumn Issue
Opinion Literary Quarterly Spring Issue
Dhondy, Farrukh and Diane Troy. Carcanet
Measure 3: Young Poets of India ,
Nandy, Pritish (Translator). Poems from Bangladesh
Anstey, F. Baboo Jabberjee
Katrak, K. D. Purgatory: Songs from The Holy Planet
Chattopadhyaya, Harindranath. The Map of the World
Banerjea, Pradeep (Translator). Some Post-Independence Bengali Poems
Shungloo, Krishan. The Night is Heavy
Chattopadhyaya, Harindranath. The Sleeper Awakened
Chattopadhyaya, Harindranath. Edgeways and the Saint
Jhabvala, S. H. Kabir
Acharya, Sri Ananda. Snow-Birds
Malgonkar, Manohar. Rumble-Tumble

Sen, Pradip. And then the Sun
Katrak, K. D. Underworld
Tripathi, Govardhanram Madhavram. Scrap Book

Hutheesing, Krishna. With No Regrets

scopecontent.p: Includes scans of book covers from the 1920's and 1940's

did.unittitle: Adi Katrak and Ajit Singh Correspondence and Carcanet Issue

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original and copy of Adil Jussawalla's birth certificate, both of which are restricted until his death.

did.unittitle: Official Correspondence from colleges and schools

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There is only one letter from 2006, the vast majority date to 1963

Comments and reviews of Clearing House books

Clearing House - Letters from Different Years

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Continuation of dealings with Indian Express, as Sunday Standard is its supplement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes packets of unpublished works

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Gauri Deshpande's letters regarding the "boycott on a USIS programme taking part in 1971" (Bangladesh Liberation War)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an apology letter addressed to "Farida Peddar" (1986)

did.unittitle: Correspondence concerning paintings/painters, Bhuphen, etc. and Articles on War from 1999

New Statesman and The Century on Commonwealth Literature

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two correspondences and one clipping detailed "Last Summer"

did.unittitle: Advisory panel for the Central Board of Film
Concerning my attempts to start teaching an English-language Course for Students in Schools and Colleges in Bombay and a Composition by One of the Students

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies.

did.unittitle: Sense and Nonsense About English in India and Pakistan
Short study "The Mountain" from Oxford days
E.M. Forster copies of letters (1959) originals with Kings College, Cambridge
Background 2 on World I & II - India
More on Clearing House and Praxis
On poets Fredoon Kabraji and Beram Saklatvala some of their work and notices
Concerning Dosebai Cowasjee Jessawalla, great-grand-aunt, authors of the stoy of my life, Bombay, Times of India Press, 1911
Concerning Veronik's grandfather Pierre Quainon and his memoir of the First World War
Concerning Almost Island its programmes, its Indo-China dialogue
On my father Dr. J.M. Jussawalla and family tree
Background concerning the Sahitya Akademi, the British Councul. The Activity High School
Original and copies of "Utopias for the Houseless"…copy of editorial I wrote when Amit Chauahuri asked me…original asked me…original of "Six Authors in Search of a Reader", illustrated
Rathnakar Kini drafts of poems, early 1960s
Covers of Clearing House books (of Distance in Statute Miles missing) plus second cover of Land's End, Not Writers Workshops, designed by Chris Oastler
Jeet Thayil. the interview with Jeet Thayil by Nilanjana S. Roy and review of his Fulcrum Anthology by Meera Visanathan
Sudesh Mishra on Gieve Patel, Jayantha Mahapatra, Kamala Das, and me from Preparing Faces by Sudesh Mishra

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopy.

did.unittitle: Rathnakar Kini some of his journalism, early 1960s
From University College Record, October 1992, On the Sheeley Bicentennial as Celebrated in College and an article I wrote on the subject
Copies of Kishore Theckedath's letter and postcards from prison 1974-76, Theckedath was a leading member of Bombay University Teacher's Union
Dom Moraes -- two articles many of Dom's are with SCILET, American College, Madurai

Wasafiri -- poems, including some by "Bombay poets". Nether -- two interviews of poets…my article on Manhar Shetty. My review of Arvind Krishna Mehrotra's Pomes, poemes, Poemas (damaged) and one of my poems used in the Anthology Recess…
Rathnakar Kini, drafts of fictionm early 1960s, this includes a note on Rathanakar
An article I wrote in 1972 and sone responses to it. Times Weekly, a supplement of the Times of India, was edited by Darryl D'Monte
The Ezekiels -- on Daisy, on Khorshed Wadia Ezekiel plus my introduction to Nissim's selected prose
Cathedral School, Bombay -- background, photographs, Class of '55
Background 1. Miscellaneous events in Bombay. Photographs, comments on writers, etc.
Me being interviewed or written plus brief opinions
Readings and events in Bombay in ehwhich some poets take part
Copies of my early poems (1955 onward) not published in a book
Typescript of Bombay-based bilingual writer (Marathi and English) damodar Prabhu's novel (eBook published by Poetrywala)
Lists of what I sent to SCILET made by the staff at SCILET (incomplete)
Karavan's 20th anniversary issue (Swedish). It carries interviews with Meena Alexander, Anjum Hasan, and me
The Beloit Poetry Journal -- Contemporary Poetry: a Chapbook, Winter 1962-1963
A few letters from my relatives
One of my articles on Dilip Chitre
Caricatures of me and a portrait
Photographs from International Writing Program and of Gieve and Arvind at a Literary Festival in Bombay (Tata Literary Festival?)
Copies of my letters to poets and writers…

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Undated photocopies of letters from 2005-2017.

did.unittitle: A copy of my letter to Khushwant informing him of mother's death and a copy of his reply and a report in Parisana
Concerning Toni Patel (an event I organized to commemorate her after her death) and concerning Santan Rodrigues
Notices of my books or contributions to anthologies and Anand Thakore on missing person
Concerning my appointment as guest lecturer in English Department recommendations from Nissim Ezekiel and Eunice De Souza
Concerning Anand Thakor. Reviews, an article, letters, events
Background 4: concerning three important figures: Beram Saklatvala….KamaiWood….Beram Malabam
My acceptance speech at Tata Lit Life Poet Laureate Award Ceremony 20 Nov. 2021 plus acceptance speech for Sahitya Akademi Award (2014)
Concerning Nissim Ezekiel R-Raj Roo underated
More on Clearing House, includes a photograph
Letters, cards from friends and others, 1970s onwards and copy of my reply to Sudhir Sonalkar after he wrote about one of my published articles
Concerning my appointment as guest lecturer, University of Bombay and letters of recommendation by Nissim and Eunice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Undated photocopies of letters from 1983.

did.unittitle: Concerning Veronik -- Veronik's translations in French for TELCO and for Western Outdoor Studio, Bombay
Concerning Veronik -- a note on some of her teachers and write-ups on one of them -- Nina Tikanova and 3 more of my cards addressed to her
Concerning Rathakar Kini -- more of his writings (they include a travelogue, verses, and fragments)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes undated photograph and typed note from Harold Lloyd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Produced for Channel 4 television as War by Jeff Pesko. Telecast 1989. Narrated by Michael Hodem and, towards the end, by Tom Watt, Verse narrative written by Adil Jussawalla.
